skill.name: crewai
skill.description: Build role-based CrewAI crews with guidance for agents, tasks, orchestration, process selection, planning, memory, tools, and flows.
skill.summary: Design structured CrewAI teams for collaborative multi-agent workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns. AI review found these were false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, and ordinary CrewAI prose or examples. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious execution, credential theft, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown code fences and inline code formatting in instructional examples. No shell command execution, Ruby backtick execution, or command injection path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown delimiters or inline skill names, not executable code. The surrounding content is documentation for Python CrewAI examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alerts on Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts reference front matter, agent role descriptions, task descriptions, and comments. No weak hash, cipher, certificate handling, or cryptographic API usage was present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited lines contain natural language or YAML task text. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic implementation or insecure algorithm selection.

content.value_statement: CrewAI projects can become unclear when agents, tasks, and processes are not defined precisely. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design structured role-based crews with practical orchestration patterns.
actual_capabilities: Defines CrewAI agents with roles, goals, and backstories.
Designs task dependencies and expected outputs for crew workflows.
Explains when to use sequential or hierarchical CrewAI processes.
Guides planning configuration for complex multi-agent execution.
Identifies common CrewAI anti-patterns and better alternatives.
limitations: Requires Python 3.10 or newer and the CrewAI package.
Assumes access to an LLM provider for running crews.
Focuses on structured workflows rather than simple one-step prompts.
Provides guidance and examples, not a complete production application.

use_cases.title: Plan a Research and Writing Crew
use_cases.description: Create clear researcher, analyst, and writer responsibilities with chained task outputs.

use_cases.title: Prototype Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Convert a product idea into CrewAI agents, tasks, process type, and execution flow.

use_cases.title: Review CrewAI Designs
use_cases.description: Find vague roles, missing expected outputs, and unnecessary agents before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Crew
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new CrewAI project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a simple CrewAI crew for [workflow]. Include agent roles, goals, task descriptions, expected outputs, and the best process type.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Agent Roles
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these CrewAI agents and tasks: [paste design]. Make each role specific, remove overlap, and improve each expected output.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Orchestration Process
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare sequential and hierarchical CrewAI processes for [project]. Recommend one option and explain the tradeoffs for coordination, speed, and output quality.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Advanced Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for complex agent collaboration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Architect a CrewAI workflow for [complex objective]. Include specialized agents, task dependencies, planning needs, memory considerations, tool usage, and validation checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Design a crew for producing a market brief.
output_examples.output: A structured crew plan with researcher, analyst, and writer agents, ordered tasks, expected deliverables, and a recommended sequential process.

output_examples.input: Review my CrewAI design with six overlapping agents.
output_examples.output: A concise review that merges duplicate roles, clarifies ownership, and replaces vague outputs with measurable completion criteria.

output_examples.input: Decide whether my workflow needs a manager agent.
output_examples.output: A process recommendation that explains when hierarchical coordination is useful and when sequential execution is simpler.

best_practices: Give every agent a narrow role, clear goal, and relevant backstory.
Define expected outputs before connecting tasks together.
Use three to five agents unless the workflow clearly needs more.
anti_patterns: Using broad roles such as developer or analyst without a specialty.
Creating tasks without done criteria or expected output formats.
Adding agents for simple steps that a tool or one task can handle.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps design CrewAI multi-agent workflows with clear roles, tasks, processes, and outputs.

faq.question: Does it run CrewAI projects directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and implementation patterns for projects that use CrewAI.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use a hierarchical process?
faq.answer: Use it when a manager agent must coordinate specialized agents across complex tasks.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide the workflow goal, available tools, desired outputs, constraints, and any existing agent design.

faq.question: What should I review before deployment?
faq.answer: Check API access, tool permissions, task outputs, retry behavior, logging, and data handling requirements.
